module.name: pinstore
module.spec: FileStateStore.Load reads .drift/state.xml via the caller-provided fileio.Session (see fileio.session), unmarshals XML into Specs, Markers, and Edges, and returns the State. State.xml version=4 is the provenance-closure format: baseline only, no <edgeResolutions>. Pre-v4 files are refused with ErrStateVersionUnsupported, directing the user to delete .drift/ and re-init. If the file does not exist, it returns ErrStateNotFound.

FileStateStore.Save marshals State to indented XML and writes it to .drift/state.xml via the caller-provided fileio.Session (see fileio.write_atomicity), which atomically replaces any existing file via temp-file + rename. The XML root element is drift (version 4) with nested specs, markers, and edges sections. Save does not create directories or take locks; the Session owns both concerns.
When .drift/state.xml does not exist on disk, Load returns ErrStateNotFound, prompting the user to run drift init first.

BaselineStore manages content-addressed baseline content packed in a single .drift/baselines.bin file, gob-encoded as a map[string]string (hash → content). For markers, the hash is sha1(content). For specs, the hash is the canonical hash (sha1 of spec content with <ref> tags stripped, see scanner.spec_discovery); the stored content is the raw innerxml including refs so that drift diff and drift show display faithful spec text. The legacy sha1(content)==hash integrity check is therefore not enforced — drift's own scanner produces matching pairs, and the display concern outweighs the integrity net for spec baselines. Read(sess, hash) loads the packfile via Session.Read, unmarshals, and returns content for hash or false if missing. Write(sess, hash, content) loads the packfile, updates the map, marshals, and atomic-writes via Session.Write (see fileio.write_atomicity). Delete(sess, hash) removes the entry and persists. All three operations route through the caller-provided fileio.Session; the Session's lock serializes concurrent writers. See principles.specs_are_truth — content-addressed by canonical hash, and see fileio.session.
